experiment: DOMEX-CAT-S459 — FM-28 hardening: automated preemption detection in task_order.py
method: Added _extract_task_anchors() and _check_preemption() to task_order.py. Anchor extraction parses file names, lesson IDs, lane IDs, frontier/FM/SIG IDs from task action strings. Preemption check compares anchors against git log --oneline -5 + git diff --name-only HEAD~5..HEAD. Tasks with >=50% anchor overlap are marked [PREEMPTED] and score -50. When >50% of actionable tasks preempted, injects NOVEL-tier redirect.
expect: task_order.py gains preemption filter: checks git log against DUE items, marks preempted tasks, suggests novel work when >50% preempted. FM-28 UNMITIGATED->MINIMAL.
actual: Implemented: 3 additions to task_order.py: (1) _extract_task_anchors() extracts file/ID anchors from task strings, (2) _check_preemption() matches anchors against recent commits, marks [PREEMPTED] with score penalty, (3) NOVEL-tier injection when >50% actionable tasks preempted. Tested: INDEX.md DUE item correctly preempted (recent concurrent commit compacted it). L-1117.md and DOMEX-BRN-S458 correctly not preempted (commit messages didn't contain specific anchors). Self-application: this session experienced 3/3 DUE items preempted by concurrent sessions — the tool would have detected and redirected faster.
diff: Expected: preemption filter + FM-28 MINIMAL. Actual: CONFIRMED — preemption filter implemented and tested (1/3 synthetic tasks correctly flagged from live git state). FM-28 gains 1 automated advisory layer (task_order.py). UNMITIGATED->MINIMAL. Swiss Cheese gap: needs 2nd layer (orient.py session-start preemption scan) for ADEQUATE.

defenses: task_order.py _check_preemption() — advisory [PREEMPTED] tag on tasks with >=50% anchor overlap against recent 5 commits (S459)
Rule: re-check git log --oneline -3 before each task execution (L-526)

test_results.novel_tier_injection: confirmed in logic (fires when >50% actionable tasks preempted)

data.upgrade_path: Add orient.py session-start preemption scan: on orient, check if DUE items from maintenance.py were addressed in commits since last session. This would provide earlier detection (at orient time, not task_order time). Low effort.
